strengths: direct standings endpoint instead of crawling
league/season parameters can become deterministic contracts
row validation can use existing W/D/L/points/GD gates
can support many leagues if league-id mapping is governed
blockers: free tier/rate limits constrain bulk execution
historical season availability must be measured per league
provider data is not official-authority by default; needs source-policy decision

strengths: works immediately in demo with test key
good for proving provider adapter interface
blockers: test key is not production policy

strengths: SQLite cache and rate limiter already modeled
same external interface despite different provider APIs
matches the family-only pivot better than crawler/discovery
blockers: Python demo is not directly compatible with current Node/ESM engine jobs
models are betting-site oriented, not football-truth lifecycle oriented
must not bypass truth validation gates
must not be treated as official source without policy
requiredAdaptations: translate provider abstraction to Node family adapter contract
add provider provenance and licensing/rate-limit metadata
